Title: Gap-free genome assemblies of two Pyrus bretschneideri cultivars and GWAS analyses identify a CCCH zinc finger protein as a key regulator of stone cell formation in pear fruit

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core fruit lignification toolbox; monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in fruit lignification, supported in this paper as a direct regulatory target of PbdsZF
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids by ligating them to CoA to form hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to stone cell formation and fruit texture; structural support
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct pathway intermediates toward monolignol and lignin production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during early fruit development; especially lignifying stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically/highly expressed during early pear fruit development; decreases as fruit matures
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pear fruit stone cells/flesh lignified regions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA867130; PRJNA588520; PRJNA825067; CNSA CNP0003413
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis associated with stone cell formation when transcriptionally activated by PbdsZF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative phylogeny; RNA-seq expression profiling; RT-qPCR after PbdsZF overexpression/silencing; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pbds4CL4 was identified as one of 14 core fruit lignification genes in the DS genome. 2. Its expression is high in early fruit development, coinciding with lignin/stone cell formation. 3. PbdsZF directly binds CT-rich motifs in the Pbds4CL4 promoter and activates its transcription, supporting increased lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for reducing stone cell content and improving pear fruit texture by promoter editing or lowering expression; also candidate for stacking with regulatory genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains CT-rich motifs CT1/CT2-type recognized by PbdsZF; directly implicated in pear fruit lignification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient agroinfiltration in fruit; transient reporter assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Pbds4CL4 promoter in assays; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter used for PbdsZF overexpression construct

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core fruit lignification toolbox; monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in fruit lignification, supported in this paper as a direct regulatory target of PbdsZF
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to the corresponding hydroxycinnamaldehydes in monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to stone cell formation and fruit texture; structural support
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate monolignol precursors toward lignin production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed during early fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically/highly expressed during early pear fruit development; decreases during maturation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pear fruit stone cells/flesh lignified regions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA867130; PRJNA588520; PRJNA825067; CNSA CNP0003413
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis associated with stone cell formation when transcriptionally activated by PbdsZF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative phylogeny; RNA-seq expression profiling; RT-qPCR after PbdsZF overexpression/silencing; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbdsCCR2 was identified as one of 14 core fruit lignification genes in the DS genome. 2. It is highly expressed in early fruit development during lignin accumulation. 3. PbdsZF directly binds CT-rich motifs in the PbdsCCR2 promoter and activates its transcription, linking it to stone cell-associated lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for reducing lignin and stone cell content via promoter editing or transcriptional modulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains CT-rich motifs bound by PbdsZF; directly implicated in fruit lignification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; transient agroinfiltration in fruit; transient reporter assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PbdsCCR2 promoter in assays; CaMV 35S constitutive promoter used for PbdsZF overexpression construct

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-TZF CCCH zinc finger protein with C-X7-C-X5-C-X3-H motif and RNA recognition motif (RRM)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Builds stone cells affecting pear fruit texture and structural reinforcement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin biosynthesis and stone cell formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation by direct promoter binding to CT-rich motifs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Pbds4CL4; PbdsCCR2; transcript levels of PbdsPAL1; PbdsPAL4; PbdsC3H4; PbdsC4H5; PbdsF5H3; Pbds4CL2; Pbds4CL4; PbdsHCT2; PbdsCSE2; PbdsCCoAOMT1; PbdsCOMT8; PbdsCCR1; PbdsCCR2; PbdsCAD1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Pbds4CL4 and PbdsCCR2 directly; positively affects expression of multiple lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit developmental stage; GWAS-associated SNP/haplotype on Chr3 linked to stone cell content
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in seeds, roots, styles, stamens, flower buds, ovaries, and young fruits (25-55 DAF); low in mature/older fruit tissues and vegetative tissues listed in paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during early fruit development (25-55 DAF), coinciding with lignin accumulation and stone cell formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pear fruit stone cells/flesh lignified regions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and stone cell formation in pear fruit
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator of stone cell formation identified by GWAS and functional validation; directly activates core lignification genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype association; RNA-seq expression analysis; subcellular localization; in situ hybridization; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing; lignin and stone cell quantification; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing/GWAS: PRJNA563813; RNA-seq: PRJNA867130; PRJNA588520; PRJNA825067; CNSA CNP0003413; genome assemblies GSA: CRA016243 and CRA016227
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified PbdsZF as a candidate gene at a major stone cell-associated locus on chromosome 3. 2. PbdsZF is highly expressed in early fruit development when lignin and stone cells accumulate. 3. Nuclear-localized PbdsZF overexpression increases, whereas silencing decreases, lignin and stone cell content in pear fruit. 4. PbdsZF positively alters expression of multiple core lignification genes. 5. PbdsZF directly binds CT-rich motifs in Pbds4CL4 and PbdsCCR2 promoters and activates their transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for breeding/editing to modulate stone cell content and improve pear texture; promoter/coding variation can be exploited in marker-assisted selection or genome editing
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in pCAMBIA1301-35S for overexpression; pGreenII62-SK effector system in dual-luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a novel non-tandem zinc finger (non-TZF) gene; associated SNP at Chr3:20,802,799 (C/G) linked to increased stone cell content, with heterozygous genotype showing strongest effect
